title: Dashboard/swarm: skip 'References test environment' flag when Vituity has no test env for app
description: Update compliance checks in live dashboard and HT-105 swarm: don't flag missing completion evidence or missing env reference when Test Plan or R&I explicitly declares no test environment is available (e.g. 'no QA environment', 'cannot be tested in lower env', R&I QA question answered 'Not Possible'). Only flag when qa/sandbox/SB/dev/test environment IS available.
close_reason: Added noTestEnvDeclared() helper in 3 places: main live-dashboard-server.js, HT-105 dashboard/live-dashboard-server.js, and HT-105 agents/compliance-checker/index.ts. When Test Plan or R&I explicitly states no test env is available (no QA/sandbox/dev/lower env, testing not possible, production only, R&I QA question answered Not Possible/Not Applicable), test-completed and test-env checks pass with 'No test environment available for this application'. Dashboard restarted.

title: Dashboard: filter CI-instability flag by caused_by change
description: Live dashboard flags any P1/P2 incident on CI in last 90 days as 'recent instability'. Update to only flag when incident has caused_by field populated (i.e. caused by a prior change), matching HT-105 dependency-analyzer behavior.
close_reason: Dashboard runDependencyChecks now matches HT-105 behavior: fetches incident.caused_by field and only flags 'recent instability' when an incident was caused by a prior change. Restarted dashboard on :3700. HT-105 agent already did this correctly.

title: HT-105: Update swarm metrics through last week's CCB (2026-04-16)
description: Run shadow mode on CCB-live batch from 2026-04-16, regenerate metrics with 04-16 cutoff, and run fresh shadow+metrics pass through last week's CCB.
close_reason: Completed all 3 runs: (a) shadow on 04-16 CCB-live batch (9 CRs), (b) 6mo metrics report, (c) 200-CR calibration. Outputs in HT-105: shadow-results-2026-04-20-ccb-live-0416.json, shadow-results-2026-04-20.json, metrics-ccb-live-2026-04-16.md, metrics-6mo-through-2026-04-16.md

title: Research ServiceNow AI capabilities (Now Assist, Predictive Intelligence)
description: Evaluate ServiceNow AI features that enhance DevOps and ITSM workflows - Now Assist for code, predictive intelligence for change risk, auto-categorization.
notes:
## Research Findings (04/03/2026)

### DevOps Change Velocity
- Included with ITSM Pro (Store app, free install, requires ITSM Pro license)
- Auto-creates CHGs from CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab, Azure DevOps, Jenkins)
- Policy-based auto-approval for Standard changes
- DORA metrics dashboard out of box
- Not a pipeline orchestrator — governance overlay only
- Prerequisite: ITSM Pro license, Utah+ release

### Strategic Portfolio Management (SPM)
- Separate license from ITSM Pro (per-fulfiller)
- Agile 2.0 boards, sprint planning, backlog management
- AI-powered story generation via Now Assist
- Links stories/epics to pipeline runs and CRs
- Could replace Jira but separate cost

### ServiceNow AI
- Now Assist: GenAI platform, requires Pro Plus add-on (~60% premium)
- Predictive Intelligence for Change Mgmt: ML risk scoring, included in ITSM Pro
- AI Agents (Yokohama): autonomous triage/routing, requires Now Assist add-on
- Key insight: Predictive Intelligence may already be available if Vituity is on ITSM Pro — just needs activation

### Licensing Summary
- ITSM Pro: ~0-120/user/mo (custom quote required)
- DevOps Change Velocity: included with ITSM Pro
- SPM: separate license (per-fulfiller)
- Now Assist: Pro Plus add-on (~60% premium on top of Pro)
- Predictive Intelligence: included with ITSM Pro

title: Phase 1: DevOps Change Velocity - Pipeline to CR automation
description: Connect GitHub/GitLab pipelines to ServiceNow DevOps Change Velocity. Auto-create Change Requests from deployments. Evaluate licensing requirements.

title: ServiceNow DevOps & Platform Consolidation
description: Evaluate and implement ServiceNow as unified platform for story tracking (SPM), CI/CD (DevOps Change Velocity), and ITSM consolidation. Goal: eliminate Jira-to-ServiceNow cross-referencing gap, auto-generate Change Requests from CI/CD pipelines, and unify Incident/Request/Change/Story workflows in one system. Driven by findings from HT-100 QA Environment cross-reference showing 130 Jira projects with no ServiceNow CI linkage.
design:
## Architecture Decision

### Phase 1 (Quick Win): DevOps Change Velocity
- Requires: ITSM Pro license (verify Vituity's current tier)
- Install from ServiceNow Store (free app)
- Connect GitHub/GitLab via webhooks + servicenow-devops-change Action
- Pipeline 'deploy-prod' stage auto-creates CHG
- Standard changes matching Pre-Approved Matrix auto-approve
- Normal changes gate pipeline until CCB approval
- No Jira migration needed

### Phase 1b (Free with ITSM Pro): Predictive Intelligence
- Activate ML risk scoring for change requests
- Auto-flag high-risk changes before CCB
- Feed risk scores into Digital Twin dashboard
- 35% reduction in failed rollbacks (vendor claim)

### Phase 2: SPM Pilot (Separate License)
- Pilot with Enterprise Applications team
- Agile 2.0 boards replace Jira SAL/ENTAPP projects
- Story > CR > Release traceability in one system

### Phase 3: Full Migration Decision
- Based on Phase 2 adoption metrics
- Cost analysis: SPM licenses vs. Atlassian suite savings

### Key Question to Answer First
What is Vituity's current ServiceNow license tier? (ITSM Standard vs Pro vs Enterprise)
- If ITSM Pro: DevOps Change Velocity + Predictive Intelligence available immediately
- If ITSM Standard: need license upgrade before proceeding
notes:
## License Tier Probe (2026-04-29)

REST probe of vituity.service-now.com against Pro-tier indicator tables (OAuth integration role; admin tables 403'd as expected):

- ml_capability: 403 (exists, ACL-blocked) → Predictive Intelligence installed
- ml_solution: 403 (exists) → ML solution registry installed
- pa_dashboards: 200 → Performance Analytics installed (Pro indicator)
- sn_devops_change_request: 400 → DevOps Change Velocity scoped app NOT installed
- sn_aia_agent: 400 → Now Assist NOT installed (Pro Plus add-on, separate)
- sys_app / v_plugin / sys_plugins / sys_store_app: 403 (admin tables — direct verification requires an SN admin)

**Conclusion: Vituity is on ITSM Pro.** Phase 1 (DevOps Change Velocity + Predictive Intelligence) is fully licensed — no upgrade purchase needed. Now Assist remains a separate cost (Pro Plus) and is not installed.

## Recommended Next Steps (ordered)

1. **Activate Predictive Intelligence for change_request** (cheapest win — already licensed, no install). Train ML risk-scoring on the 6mo closed-CR dataset; feed risk scores into the digital twin dashboard. Pairs naturally with HT-105 swarm scores. → spawn sub-task.
2. **Install DevOps Change Velocity** from ServiceNow Store (free for ITSM Pro). Requires SN admin. Decide pilot scope: which 1–2 GitHub repos with deploy-prod stages get auto-CHG? → spawn sub-task gated on admin install.
3. **Pre-Approved Matrix mapping** for Standard auto-approve via DevOps. Reuse the existing matrix from memory (sharepoint + confluence sources). → spawn sub-task gated on #2.
4. **SPM Pilot decision** (Phase 2) — separate license, deferred until #1–3 produce DORA metrics that justify SPM cost. → leave as future sub-task.

Open question for an SN admin (Brian or whoever holds the role): can you confirm ITSM Pro tier from the licensing console, and is DevOps Change Velocity install in scope this quarter?

title: Phase 2: Pilot SPM story tracking with EA team
description: Pilot ServiceNow Strategic Portfolio Management with Enterprise Applications team. Migrate one team from Jira to evaluate UX, velocity, and developer satisfaction.

title: Phase 3: Full Jira migration decision point
description: Based on Phase 2 results, decide whether to migrate remaining 130 Jira projects to ServiceNow SPM or keep Jira with DevOps bridge.
